issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://www.liveasystreetapartments.com/?rcstdid=Mg%3D%3D-YRjTEizZPgM%3D](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.liveasystreetapartments.com%2F%3Frcstdid%3DMg%253D%253D-YRjTEizZPgM%253D) ### Comment > I don’t appreciate all the work that I’m having to put in to try to get this problem to you guys. This is a whole lot of hoops to jump through and way way too much work that guard has broken Safari. That’s all I know AdGuard has broken Safari in iOS and it’s rendering it unusable. The only thing I can use Safari with now is watching YouTube videos. Everything else is absolutely worthless. I can’t refresh screens entire website. Just freeze screens are unresponsive and the moment I turned AdGuard off everything is better immediately. ### Screenshots
Screenshot 1 ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/o6hdd0akksgwkcggg0w0k04sso4s8csaymku3ijy28.jpg?nc=1)

AdGuard Base filter,
AdGuard Mobile Ads filter

**Privacy:**
AdGuard Tracking Protection filter DNS filtering: | server: `https://unfiltered.adguard-dns.com/dns-query`
filters: `https://filters.adtidy.org/dns/filter_1_ios.txt` Other extensions used: | `Mapper, waze`

comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

issue.title: mobile-legends.fandom.com
issue.body:
### Issue URL (Annoyance) [https://mobile-legends.fandom.com/wiki/List_of_heroes](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fmobile-legends.fandom.com%2Fwiki%2FList_of_heroes) ### Comment > Go to https://mobile-legends.fandom.com/wiki/List_of_heroes. > > Get an error: "Error: Failed to load website properly since html-load.com is blocked. Please allow html-load.com." > > The error also shows up on other websites. comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 7 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://www.paramountplus.com/movies/video/6_YWAktXgebdiSgeOmcegpTJouXhh2Ql/#open](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.paramountplus.com%2Fmovies%2Fvideo%2F6_YWAktXgebdiSgeOmcegpTJouXhh2Ql%2F%23open) ### Comment > ### Issue: Required Paramount Plus domains fail to resolve when AdGuard is enabled > > When AdGuard system‑wide protection is enabled (via the system tray), several required Paramount Plus domains fail to resolve. Disabling AdGuard only in the browser does not resolve the issue — DNS resolution continues to fail until AdGuard is fully disabled at the system level. > > ### Observed behavior > - DNS lookups for required Paramount Plus domains return **REFUSED** when AdGuard system‑wide protection is active. > - Browser‑level AdGuard disabling has no effect. > - Once AdGuard is disabled system‑wide, DNS resolution succeeds and Paramount Plus begins working normally. > > ### Impact > Attempting to play any content on Paramount Plus results in: > - The player spinning for several seconds > - Playback failing and returning to the play button > - Playback working normally once AdGuard system‑wide protection is disabled > > ### Additional details > Screenshots attached showing DNS refusal for one of the required domains. > ### Screenshots

issue.body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] Filters were updated before reproducing an issue;
- [x] AdGuard product version is up-to-date;
- [x] Browser version is up-to-date;
- [x] If the site or app is broken, disabling AdGuard protection resolves an issue.

### What product do you use?

AdGuard for iOS

### AdGuard version

unknown

### What type of problem have you encountered?

Website or app doesn't work properly

### Which browser(s) do you use?

Safari

### Which device do you use?

Mobile

### Where is the problem encountered?

https://www.gopuff.com/

### Ad Blocking

AdGuard Base filter, AdGuard Mobile Ads filter

### Privacy

_No response_

### Social Widgets

_No response_

### Annoyances

_No response_

### Security

_No response_

### Other

_No response_

### DNS filters

_No response_

### Language-specific

_No response_

### What Stealth Mode options do you have enabled?

_No response_

### Add your comment and screenshots

We've received customer reports that AdGuard is blocking our session enrichment script. Spur is an IP intelligence platform focused on protecting websites, not serving advertisements. Our Monocle product helps protect sites from bots and other security threats, so blocking it degrades the protection our customers rely on.

Could you please review and remove our script domains from your filter lists?

Domains to unblock:
- [mcl.io](http://mcl.io/)
- [mcl.spur.us](http://mcl.spur.us/)

About us:
- Company: https://spur.us/
- Monocle product page: https://spur.us/platform/session-enrichment
- Monocle documentation: https://docs.spur.us/monocle


### Privacy

- [x] I agree to follow this condition

comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 4 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://www.hulu.com/watch/a7976d43-3985-4dc2-9099-ec1767d6a031](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.hulu.com%2Fwatch%2Fa7976d43-3985-4dc2-9099-ec1767d6a031) ### Comment > It used to be that using Adguard on Hulu let you fast forward through commercials and the captions and pic would get out of sync, and you'd have to click "watch credits" in order to see the end of the episode because things would be, by the end of a show, 4-5 minutes out of sync. Now with Adguard running you can still ffwd through commercials, but the "out of sync" problem makes it so the episode will end early, sometimes just cutting off a few minutes sometimes cutting off over 5 minutes (whatever is after the last commercial). It's inconsistent how much time is lost but with AdGuard running there's no way to see the last few minutes of the episode, you have to turn it off, let it reload, watch a few commercials, in order to see the last few minutes of each episode. Sorry I might not have answered a lot of the questions correctly or given you screen shots that are useful -- the questions you ask in order to submit an issue are so extremely technical (overly technical, frankly, to report a problem with a service I pay for). ### Screenshots
Screenshot 1 ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/96ytwyn2d2www4oc4goc8gowssskwkk1ny2gim1acs.png?nc=1)
Screenshot 1 with AdGuard disabled ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/cogiltfb4xcskwgkwgg4wcg48k4os8w5t71uub8wr4.png?nc=1)

This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

comment.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://www.twitch.tv/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.twitch.tv%2F) ### Comment > AdGuard causes Twitch to show "Browser not supported" when using Firefox Developer Edition. This is because Twitch's bot detection is kicking in thinking you are a bot ("failed integrity check" error appears from network requests if you are already logged in and try do different actions, and a more vague "browser not supported" for when you are logged out). > > Twitch uses Kasada bot detection and appears to be blocking or otherwise disrupting the results of those browser checks.
